item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Delightful. (Never thought I'd say that about an insect scip.) This reminds me of various Series I scips, ones that don't offer much explicit insight into how the information in the article was obtained or deduced. The document just presents a weird thing in the universe that is somehow thoroughly understood by the Foundation; the scip's strength is the elegant simplicity and quirky cleverness of its weird idea.

I think that's a great approach even when aiming to fulfill modern standards. Arguably, a realistic containment procedures writeup is unlikely to contain the addenda and experiment logs that a lot of modern SCPs rely on to tell their story (because a realistic containment procedures writeup wouldn't be trying to tell a story).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
First of all, I upvoted, so don't take at all my criticism as me not liking the piece altogether, but I do have a lot of nitpicks here that almost made me hold on that vote.

I think this piece jumps some steps unnecessarily by making casual use of some advanced parlance. I am not too fond of casual use of either conceptual anomalies nor anartists for similar reasons: they make the anomalous so casual that it stops being weird to become just the Setting's Standard Magic. I like it when even minor anomalies are outside the realm of explanation and the Foundation relies on actual science.

Usually this boils down to personal preference, though. Simplifying the description that way opens the way for the piece to go further and explore other aspects of the story quicker. Also, sometimes being a casual SSM is a fundamental part of the story being told, so the very story can't be told without it.

This piece doesn't seem to be either, though. It is a straightforward article about an anomaly. A good anomaly, with a good concept, and WWS already gives it enough of a casual undertone in the idea that the anomaly just needs a little supervision to be better contained by a local NGO in the know, so it saves resources.

Bottom line, the little side story about the anartist adds nothing to the piece, and cutting it out would save a space that can be better used expanding the description, so it does less a short explanation of what is actually a complex idea, and instead shows the challenge of both the Foundation and WWS to grasp what is going on.

As a side effect, this article as it is now probably will be hard for newcomers to understand, without making clear that it is using advanced concepts of the community.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Needs more collapsibles and interview logs. Also probably at least six fake-out designations, an exploration log of a MTF getting totally owned by them, a debriefing, and a note from O5 about the Grave Importance Of It All. All in all, it failed to tell an epic story of paper-thin characters I don't care about. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am conceptually upvoting this article, let's hope the wasps don't get me this time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Would this be a vehicle for figuring out something about scp-055 though?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Your microwave is wasps, and they're stinging you right now
+1
I am happy to inform you all that SCP-3160 has hit +100! I am less happy to inform you that there are now wasps inside the rating module.

item.title: FINALLY
item.content:encoded:
IT'S SHORT.
IT'S EFFECTIVE.
IT CAN BE READ IN ONE SITTING.
IT'S INTERESTING
THANK YOU
SO MUCH.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Would the earth be considered a conceptual whole in terms of what they can implant themselves into? Food for thought.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Love the hard work put into this, well creative and actual reference to wildlife, nice work :)

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Short and sweet. Small nitpick in that all four paragraphs in the main description start with the SCP designation or 'While' but other than that, well done! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have two thoughts:

  • Containment can also be kept by housing them with SCP-2364, in emergencies.
  • Please keep any documentation away from the containment chamber. That would have unfortunate consequences for the reader of this article, potentially.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

I like it, but my one problem is that you -tell- us what the Wasps do, instead of -show- us. Instead of saying, "They disappear, and bind to an object," you simply expect us to believe the whole "conceptual" angle, without really going any deeper into how it works or why we believe that's the case. I kind of like how SCP-1539 did the whole conceptual thing better.

However, the containment method and the general concept were still enough to upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'll always enjoy a cleverly written conceptual anomaly.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the way you've phrased this. In social scientific terms we could call WWS "embedded" in a community. This article and your ohrasing have really made me excited to see where WWS will go in terms of exploring the different sorts of anomolous institutional relationships possible!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Simple but effective.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Unique and deals in complicated abstracts without going into massive technobabbles that melt your brain. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was simple and charming. The idea of finding a practical application for an anart piece is quite appealing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Really enjoyed reading this little article! It's always great to find skips like this: short, sweet and totally not going to jeopardize the integrity of the universe!

(also yay Wilson's is spreading!)

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is one of the first articles to provide a good reason as to why the Foundation would let Wilson's Wildlife Solutions operate at all - using their established status and connections to facilitate cooperation from people who would otherwise be loath to assist a clandestine extragovernmental agency.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Do I even need to comment? I can’t not like this. +1

EDIT: You know, something that is pretty great even if not intentional is that there is the blurred image of something in the background of that image, and you can kind of imagine that whatever it is that you can barely see is Object Apol. That's pretty cool.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
This is my first skip for the fantastic Wilson’s Wildlife Solutions. Thanks to Stallmantic, MaliceAF, DarkStuff, NineVolt and Westrin for their feedback!

PLEASE DON’T SUE TANHONY

The source for the image is (http://maxpixel.freegreatpicture.com/Insect-Nature-Armenia-Yellow-Jacket-2692103) under a CC0 Public Domain license (Free for commercial use, link referral required.)
